ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for roasted pumpkin seeds; SNOMED CT coverage for individual seed preparations is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of roasted pumpkin seeds is approximately 30 g (one small handful), providing around 172 kcal, 9 g protein, 14.7 g fat, and 4.4 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Roasted pumpkin seed, unsalted — Singapore HPB Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 12016)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) sourced from HPB Foodo record F09030012 at confidence 0.95. Extended micronutrients (saturated fat, MUFA, PUFA, sugar, potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, water) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pumpkin and squash seeds, roasted, without salt (FDC ID 170556 / NDB 12016), which is an exact match for this preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of seeds and nuts — analogous estimate based on pumpkin seeds; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for roasted unsalted pumpkin seeds was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for mixed seeds and nuts (GI range 14–25) from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (25 × 4.41) / 100 ≈ 1.1 per 30 g serving. The very high fat, protein, and fibre content of pumpkin seeds strongly attenuates any glycaemic response, supporting a low GI classification.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (8 mg/100 g) with high potassium (809 mg/100 g) and magnesium (592 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Roasted unsalted pumpkin seeds are highly suitable for individuals with hypertension; their negligible sodium content (8 mg/100 g), high potassium-to-sodium ratio, and substantial magnesium (592 mg/100 g) collectively support vascular tone and blood pressure regulation — limit to a 30 g serving to manage caloric density.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~25), high protein (29.84 g/100 g), high dietary fibre (6.5 g/100 g), and low sugar (1.29 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Roasted unsalted pumpkin seeds have a low estimated GI of approximately 25 and a glycemic load of only 1.1 per 30 g serving; the combination of high protein, high fibre, and high unsaturated fat substantially blunts postprandial glucose response, making them a clinically appropriate snack for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes — restrict to 30 g per occasion to limit caloric intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High unsaturated fat (MUFA 16.24 g + PUFA 20.95 g per 100 g), zero trans fat, zero cholesterol, high phytosterols, and high magnesium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Roasted unsalted pumpkin seeds provide a cardioprotective fatty acid profile dominated by monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats (37.2 g/100 g combined) with zero trans fat and zero cholesterol; their high magnesium (592 mg/100 g) and potassium (809 mg/100 g) further support cardiac function — saturated fat is moderate at 8.66 g/100 g, so a 30 g serving limit is recommended for individuals with established cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High MUFA/PUFA content, high iron (8.07 mg/100 g), high zinc (7.81 mg/100 g), and anti-inflammatory micronutrient profile
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pumpkin seeds qualify for the MUFA/PUFA clinical carve-out as whole seeds; their dominant unsaturated fat profile, high zinc (7.81 mg/100 g), high iron (8.07 mg/100 g), and vitamin E (2.18 mg/100 g) support immune regulation and joint health per EULAR dietary guidelines — pair with omega-3-rich foods to balance the omega-6 to omega-3 ratio and limit to 30 g per serving.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted pumpkin seeds (unsalted) are the dried, heat-roasted kernels of Cucurbita pepo, consumed as a nutrient-dense snack worldwide. The seeds are small, flat, and oval with a pale green to cream-coloured hull; roasting produces a lightly crunchy texture and a nutty, subtly earthy flavour without the sharpness of salt. They are sold hulled (pepitas) or with the white fibrous hull intact. Nutritionally, roasted unsalted pumpkin seeds are exceptionally rich in protein (29.84 g/100 g), total fat (49.05 g/100 g — predominantly unsaturated: MUFA 16.24 g, PUFA 20.95 g), dietary fibre (6.5 g/100 g), and key minerals including magnesium (592 mg/100 g), phosphorus (1233 mg/100 g), potassium (809 mg/100 g), iron (8.07 mg/100 g), and zinc (7.81 mg/100 g). Sodium is negligible at 8 mg/100 g. Energy density is high at 574 kcal/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 25 (low), making them suitable for blood glucose management. Clinically, they are appropriate for individuals with hypertension (low sodium, high potassium and magnesium), Type 2 Diabetes (low GI, high protein and fibre), and cardiovascular disease (cardioprotective fatty acid profile, zero cholesterol, zero trans fat). They are naturally vegan, gluten-free, halal, and kosher. A standard serving is 30 g, providing approximately 172 kcal.
common_user_queries: Are pumpkin seeds good for diabetics?
Are pumpkin seeds high in protein?

confidence_reason: Primary macros sourced directly from HPB Foodo record F09030012 at confidence 0.95. Extended micronutrients cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 170556 (pumpkin and squash seeds, roasted, without salt), which is an exact preparation match. Both sources are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC 170556) for roasted unsalted pumpkin seeds. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FDC 170556 — pumpkin seeds are a plant food with no dietary cholesterol. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero as pumpkin seeds are a plant food containing no B12. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero per USDA FDC 170556. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate based on Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) as no direct GI measurement for roasted unsalted pumpkin seeds was found in the University of Sydney GI Database; the very high fat, protein, and fibre content strongly supports a low GI classification. No -1 sentinel values require documentation beyond the standard template defaults; all extended micronutrients have been populated from USDA FDC 170556. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03301491 and FoodEx2 code A00LC are defensible anchors for pumpkin seed food products. SNOMED CT code not assigned — no specific code identified for this preparation. This food is not cuisine-specific (generic/international); no mandatory local-language names apply under G6 multilingual rules.
